html:
Benguet Corp. Class A (PSE:BC)

Benguet is not the Dizon/Siana restart story in the stale draft. The live filings point to a simpler, cleaner thesis: an old, illiquid Philippine miner with active gold, nickel, logistics, real estate, and exploration assets that is trading like a broken legacy vehicle despite 2025 earnings already proving current-cycle operating leverage.

At the May 18, 2026 PSE quote page, BC’s reference price was PHP 6.50, the May 15 previous close, with the market open and no new print shown at that timestamp. Using Benguet’s disclosed 429.6 million Class A shares and 286.0 million Class B shares, the combined common equity value is roughly PHP 4.65 billion. Against that, 2025 net income was PHP 759.8 million, revenue was PHP 3.31 billion, EPS was PHP 1.06, and book value was PHP 14.17 per share per the PSE financial summary. That is about 6.1x 2025 earnings and 0.46x book before giving credit for 2026 gold prices, a normalized nickel shipping year, or any liquidity improvement from the approved A/B share declassification.

The variant perception is that BC still looks like a dusty, governance-heavy, low-liquidity PSE mining relic. That label is partly deserved, but it hides the inflection. In H1 2025, Acupan sold 3,123 ounces of gold at an average $3,080/oz, while Sta. Cruz shipped 709,170 tons of nickel ore at $37.62/ton, driving PHP 623.9 million of first-half net income. Q3 then looked ugly because typhoons and swells halted nickel shipments and consolidated results swung to a PHP 68.3 million loss, but even with that weather hit, nine-month net income was PHP 555.5 million and full-year profit still reached PHP 760 million. That is not a promotional development story. It is a seasonal producer whose market cap is only about 6 years of already-reported earnings.

The gold lever is especially underpriced. Acupan is small, but it is high enough grade to matter: Q2 2025 mill head grades were above 8 g/t, and Q3 revenue rose as gold prices and production improved. Every $100/oz move on roughly 7,000 to 8,000 annual ounces is about PHP 40 million to PHP 45 million of revenue sensitivity before tax. Spot gold has been running materially above Benguet’s 2025 realized prices, so 2026 gold earnings can step up without any new mine fantasy. If Acupan volumes merely hold and realized prices reprice upward from the H1/Q3 2025 averages, the gold segment alone can add several hundred million pesos of pretax profit versus 2025.

The nickel side is lumpier but gives the stock torque. Sta. Cruz earned PHP 415 million in H1 2025, then hit a weather-driven Q3 loss because no nickel ore shipments were made. That is exactly why the market underwrites the company with a punitive multiple. But a normal shipping season does not need heroic nickel assumptions to matter. If Sta. Cruz repeats a first-half-like shipment cadence and nickel ore prices hold near the 2025 range, Benguet can plausibly earn PHP 900 million to PHP 1.1 billion in 2026 net income. That would put BC at roughly 4.2x to 5.2x forward earnings, with an enterprise value closer to 2x earnings after net cash and financial assets.

The asset backing is real, but it should be treated as downside support rather than the whole bull case. StockAnalysis reports about PHP 2.9 billion of cash and PHP 12.8 million of debt, while the PSE financial summary shows PHP 4.1 billion of current assets, PHP 1.6 billion of total liabilities, and PHP 10.1 billion of equity at year-end 2025. The stock is not cheap only because of cash. It is cheap because the operating business is profitable, commodity-linked, and under-earning relative to current-cycle gold, while investors are paying less than half of book value.

Capital allocation is improving, but not yet great. The company declared a PHP 0.15 common dividend payable May 13, 2026, a modest 2.3% yield at PHP 6.50 and a low payout against PHP 1.06 of 2025 EPS. That is enough to signal solvency and shareholder awareness, not enough to prove a disciplined return-of-capital culture. The cash balance becomes more valuable if it funds higher-return mine work, accelerates Pantingan/Zamboanga permitting, or supports higher dividends. It becomes less valuable if management hoards it or dribbles it into low-return legacy projects.

The underappreciated catalyst is structural cleanup. Benguet’s Class A/Class B declassification was approved by the board and stockholders in November 2025, with SEC approval still pending. A unified common share class could reduce a long-standing complexity discount, improve screenability, and make market-cap and float data less confusing. It will not magically create institutional liquidity, but it removes one reason local investors ignore the name.

The biggest near-term risk is information timing. Benguet filed a May 13, 2026 notice of inability to file the Q1 2026 report, so the market does not yet have March-quarter confirmation of whether gold strength and nickel shipments carried forward. That matters. A late filing, a weak Q1, or evidence that 2025 was a one-off could cap the rerating. Other real risks are weather-disrupted nickel shipments, Philippine permitting friction, old-mine rehabilitation liabilities, related-party or empire-building behavior, and severe gold/nickel price reversals. The stock is illiquid, so the price can overshoot both directions.

Still, the asymmetry is attractive because the current price does not require much to work. At PHP 6.50, investors are paying about 6x last year’s earnings, roughly 0.46x book, and a low single-digit EV multiple for a miner that has already restarted dividends, has net cash, and has direct operating leverage to a gold price well above its 2025 realized average. The market is pricing Benguet as a stale Philippine resource oddity. The forward case is that it is becoming a cash-rich current-cycle earnings vehicle with hidden asset value and an approaching share-class simplification.

Bear case: PHP 4.25, down 35%. Gold mean-reverts sharply, nickel shipments are disrupted again, Q1 2026 confirms weak operating momentum, and the market values Benguet at about 4x depressed EPS and 0.30x book.

Base case: PHP 12.00, up 85%. Gold stays strong, nickel shipments normalize, 2026 earnings reach roughly PHP 1.35 to PHP 1.55 per share, the dividend remains in place, and the stock rerates to 7.5x to 8x earnings or about 0.8x forward book.

Bull case: PHP 20.00, up 208%. Acupan captures much higher realized gold prices, Sta. Cruz has a normal-to-strong shipping year, 2026 to 2027 EPS approaches PHP 2.00, management raises cash returns, and the A/B share declassification improves visibility enough for the market to value Benguet at 10x earnings or a little above book.
